title: NOC 75119 – Other trades helpers and labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75119 is a versatile "catch-all" category for the essential support workforce across Canada’s technical sectors. As a TEER 5 occupation, these workers assist a wide variety of skilled tradespersons—including painters, insulators, glazers, and metal fabricators—in diverse settings such as manufacturing plants, commercial buildings, and shipyards. In 2026, as Canada revitalizes its industrial base and maritime infrastructure, these "utility" helpers are classified as Critical Operational Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level5Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group751 – Helpers and labourersMinor Group7511 – Trades helpers and labourersNOC Code75119

duties: Trade Support: Assist skilled tradespersons by moving materials and tools to the work site and providing general manual assistance.
Surface Preparation: Clean and prepare surfaces for painting or coating using scrapers, wire brushes, and sandblasters.
Material Handling: Load and unload vehicles, and move heavy metal parts or building materials to and from work areas using hand trucks or overhead cranes.
Equipment Maintenance: Clean and lubricate trade-specific machinery and tools; assist in the assembly and dismantling of scaffolding or ramps.
Installation Assistance: Hold materials (such as glass panes or insulation batts) in place for tradespersons during installation.
Site Management: Maintain a clean and safe work environment by removing debris, hazardous waste, and unused materials.
Safety: Adhere to strict safety protocols, including the use of respirators and fall-protection gear when assisting in specialized trades.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required.
On-the-Job Training: Extensive on-the-job training is provided by the employer.

Certifications (Role Specific):WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is mandatory for those handling paints, chemicals, or insulation.Fall Protection certification is required for helpers working at heights (e.g., glazier or insulator helpers).Confined Space Entry may be required for shipyard labourers.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is mandatory for those handling paints, chemicals, or insulation.
Fall Protection certification is required for helpers working at heights (e.g., glazier or insulator helpers).
Confined Space Entry may be required for shipyard labourers.
Physical stamina for manual labor and the ability to work in varying environmental conditions (noise, heat, cold, or heights) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, helpers in specialized sectors like Shipbuilding or Aircraft Maintenance often command a 15–20% premium over general labor wages due to the technical nature of the assistance.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector is experiencing a shift toward "Green Trades" (e.g., high-efficiency insulation and eco-friendly coatings), which will sustain demand for technical helpers through 2033. As senior helpers are promoted into apprenticeship programs, a constant "Backfill Demand" ensures a steady stream of job openings for newcomers.
labour_market_demand: The demand for "Other" trades helpers in 2026 is Strong and Consistent. In Ontario and Quebec, the manufacturing resurgence has created thousands of openings for helpers in metal fabrication and industrial painting. In British Columbia and Nova Scotia, the "National Shipbuilding Strategy" is driving a specific need for helpers to assist shipwrights and marine fitters. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the vacancy rate for this NOC remains 12% higher than the national average for entry-level roles, offering excellent opportunities for international applicants.
The "Gateway" Role: Gain exposure to multiple high-paying Red Seal trades, allowing you to choose a long-term career path based on your interests.
Rapid Workforce Entry: This role typically requires no prior experience or formal education, making it the fastest way for newcomers to start earning in Canada.
Diverse Industry Options: Work in sectors ranging from aircraft maintenance and marine repair to commercial painting and insulation.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 5 role is eligible for several Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) that prioritize "Essential Support" workers in the trades.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically highlighting essential trades support roles.
Saskatchewan SINP Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A direct route for workers in this NOC with a valid job offer in the province.
OINP In-Demand Skills Stream: Frequently targets helpers and labourers in regions outside the Greater Toronto Area (GTA).
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated employer in an Atlantic province.
